canonical_name: National Initiative for Developing and Harnessing Innovations
evidence_text: Official NIDHI information identifies NIDHI as DST's umbrella programme and lists its permanent startup-support components.

canonical_name: NIDHI - Promoting and Accelerating Young and Aspiring Innovators and Startups
evidence_text: The official master page explicitly describes NIDHI-PRAYAS as sector-agnostic and explains startup and innovator access through approved centres.
Official NIDHI material identifies EIR as a permanent component that reduces early-stage risk for aspiring technology entrepreneurs.
The official SSP page describes seed assistance to potential startups through supported incubators and distinguishes incubator calls from the permanent programme.
The official page separates institutional calls to establish TBIs from startup intake conducted by individual TBIs.
Official NIDHI information identifies iTBI as a permanent NIDHI component and a delivery institution for inclusive startup support.
The official page explicitly states that supported accelerator programmes may be sector-specific or sector-agnostic; individual cohort sectors must be stored on calls.
The official NIDHI component list identifies CoE as a permanent component for globally competitive startup facilities.

secondary_sectors: ClimateTech & Clean Energy; Artificial Intelligence & Data; Biotechnology & Medical Technologies; Digital Economy & AgriTech; Space Technology; Semiconductors & Electronics
evidence_text: RDIF is the permanent DST flagship fund under ANRF. It finances private-sector enterprises and startups in sunrise and strategic sectors through designated Second-Level Fund Managers such as TDB.

Official TDB material describes continuing core technology-commercialisation finance. RDIF financing through TDB as Second-Level Fund Manager is tracked separately under the Research Development and Innovation Fund.
